title: paspalum scrobiculatum var. frumentaceum
intro: Paspalum scrobiculatum var. frumentaceum, also known as red rice grass or red millet, is a type of tropical grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its numerous medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Paspalum scrobiculatum var. frumentaceum include its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used as a food source in some parts of the world.
